goal: Build a Franka pick-and-place cell using the **bundled NVIDIA PickPlaceController** (RMPflow + ParallelGripper) instead of cuRobo. Demonstrates the alternative controller path for users who don't have cuRobo configured or want simpler RMPflow-based motion. Same scene as CP-01 (table, conveyor, 4 cubes, bin) but `target_source='builtin'` invokes the per-robot bundled controller from isaacsim.robot.manipulators.examples.franka. Robot-agnostic infrastructure: builtin handler dispatches to franka/ur10/ur10e/cobotta per robot_family arg.
thoughts:
Six specifics for CP-06 (builtin controller variant of CP-01):
1) Identical scene to CP-01: 2x1x0.75m table, 3m conveyor, 4 cubes at x=-1.4..-0.65, bin at y=-0.4. Same physics setup (CPU dynamics, sleepThreshold=0, rubber on cubes).
2) target_source='builtin' invokes _gen_pick_place_builtin which imports isaacsim.robot.manipulators.examples.franka.controllers.PickPlaceController. NVIDIA's pre-tuned RMPflow + ParallelGripper.
3) robot_family='franka' explicit (auto-detect also works via USD reference path scan — looks for 'franka' or 'panda' substring).
4) Builds a separate _builtin_pp_sub_<TAG> subscription distinct from _curobo_pp_sub_<TAG>. Form-gate (verify_pickplace_pipeline) recognizes both prefixes as valid controller installations.
5) Cube queue handled by per-step state machine: read first undelivered cube within 0.95m reach, call controller.forward(picking_position, placing_position, current_joint_positions, end_effector_offset), advance on is_done().
6) Scene state setup mirrors CP-01: world.scene.add(robot) then world.reset() (initializes physics_sim_view + gripper) then PickPlaceController(gripper=robot.gripper, ...). The world.reset() step is critical — without it robot.gripper is None and controller crashes.
code:
# DomeLight — without this the viewport renders black after new_stage()
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)

# Parent Xform
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")

# Table — Cube default size=2, scale to 2x1x0.75m with top at z=0.75
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# CPU dynamics required for PhysxSurfaceVelocityAPI
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# Franka on table, oriented so cube/belt at world +Y is in robot's base +X
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# Long conveyor belt in front of robot. Apparent speed ~3x nominal due
# to PhysX stick-slip with high-friction material.
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# Cubes start OUTSIDE robot's 0.70m pick reach so belt has visible work.
for i, x in enumerate([-1.4, -1.15, -0.9, -0.65]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

# sleepThreshold=0 on all cubes
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)

# Rubber on cubes only — combined-friction with default-mu fingers
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# Bin behind robot, floor flush on table top
create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

# Pick station inside robot reach
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Builtin controller — uses NVIDIA's bundled per-robot PickPlaceController.
# Alternative to CP-01's cuRobo. Simpler, no GPU/CUDA setup needed; works any
# install where isaacsim.robot.manipulators.examples is available.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="builtin",
    robot_family="franka",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    destination_path="/World/Bin"
)
failure_modes: Skipping world.reset() before PickPlaceController init → robot.gripper is None → 'NoneType has no attribute link_names' crash. Builtin handler does world.reset() automatically.
robot_family='auto' fails to detect → pass explicit 'franka' (or 'ur10', 'ur10e', 'cobotta_pro_900'). Auto-detect scans USD reference paths for known robot substrings.
Same physics gotchas as CP-01: GPU dynamics disabled, sleepThreshold=0, rubber on cubes only, DomeLight=1000.
isaacsim.robot.manipulators.examples not installed → ModuleNotFoundError. This module is bundled with Isaac Sim 5.x but may be missing in stripped installs.
extension_notes: CP-06 is the 'parallel canonical' to CP-01: same scene, different controller path. Validates that target_source='builtin' works for Franka. CP-07 (UR10 + builtin) is blocked on surface_gripper variant — needs Sprint 3's `surface_gripper` tool to enable. CP-08 (CobottaPro900) blocked because Cobotta wrapper class isn't in this Isaac Sim install — would need custom wrapper.
delivery: Cube_1 picked from conveyor and dropped into Bin via Franka + bundled PickPlaceController
